title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {skymodelr: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres},   author = {Tyler Morgan-Wall},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://www.skymodelr.com}, }

what: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: skymodelr generates physically‑plausible sky domes night skies high‑dynamic‑range EXR images/arrays directly R. provides Hosek–Wilkie analytic sky model (optionally) 2021–22 Prague spectral sky model (‑horizon sun, altitude, wide‑spectrum support). also includes options add physically-accurate moon models phase correctly, adjusts orientation based viewers latitude longitude, accounts changes brightness due distance variation, opposition surge, atmospheric attenuation. skymodelr also includes option add accurate visible star field aligned observer location/time. Outputs lat‑long environment maps (2:1 equirectangular) can feed renderers (rayrender). generate_sky_latlong() composes full sky environment using functions generate_sky(), generate_moon_latlong(), generate_stars() specific latitude, longitude, time. default generate_sky_latlong() includes sun’s contribution, can also include stars moon setting moon = TRUE stars = TRUE.

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: plan use Prague spectral model, download coefficient dataset(s) (see download_sky_data() ).
code: # Latest version from GitHub remotes::install_github("tylermorganwall/skymodelr")

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: generate_sky() — Write/return EXR sky dome using either Hosek–Wilkie (default) Prague model. filename = NA return HDR array ‑memory (otherwise .exr written). albedo = 0.1 ground reflectance (0–1). turbidity = 3 atmospheric turbidity (1.7–10; Hosek ). elevation = 10, azimuth = 90 solar position (degrees). altitude = 0 observer altitude meters (Prague ). resolution = 2048 image height (width 2 * resolution). number_cores = 1 threads. hosek = TRUE set FALSE use Prague model; Prague options wide_spectrum, visibility. render_mode = "" atmosphere + solar disk; use "atmosphere" omit disk "sun" disk . generate_sky_latlong() — Produce complete equirectangular sky array/EXR. Accepts date/time observer location, (optionally) adds stars moon‑lit atmosphere. Core args: filename = NA, datetime, lat, lon, albedo, turbidity, resolution, number_cores. Model selection: hosek = TRUE (Hosek–Wilkie) set hosek = FALSE use Prague spectral model; Prague extras: wide_spectrum, visibility, altitude. Composition: stars = FALSE, star_width = 1, moon = FALSE. generate_moon_latlong() — Produce moon‑lit atmosphere scaling sky dome moon’s luminance (phase + opposition surge). Computes moon’s position time/location, exposes moon-specific controls moon_extinction_kV, earthshine_albedo, solar_irradiance_w_m2. generate_stars() — Generate star‑field EXR aligned sky dome: filename = NA, resolution = 2048. lon, lat observer longitude/latitude (deg) datetime (used local sidereal time). Optional extinction/appearance controls: turbidity, ozone_du, altitude, star_width, atmosphere_effects, upper_hemisphere_only, number_cores. calculate_sky_values() — Sample radiance Prague model given sky directions (phi, theta) conditions (elevation, altitude, visibility, albedo, azimuth). download_sky_data(sea_level = TRUE, wide_spectrum = FALSE) — Download Prague model coefficient data: Sea‑level, standard spectrum: SkyModelDatasetGround.dat (~107 MB) Sea‑level, wide spectrum: PragueSkyModelDatasetGroundInfra.dat (~574 MB) Full‑altitude dataset: SkyModelDataset.dat (~2.4 GB)

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: ’ll generate sun morning (right sunrise) Washington DC March 21st. day sun rising directly east (90 degrees azimuth), can see sun appearing quarter way left side image.  Afternoon DC:  Evening DC:  Evening DC (Hosek model), note unphysical yellowish tint:  Prague model supports solar elevations horizon:  Prague model also supports altitudes 15,000m.
code: library(skymodelr) library(rayimage)  env = generate_sky_latlong(   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 06:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE ) rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure=-2) |>    rayimage::plot_image() env = generate_sky_latlong(   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 12:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE ) rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure=-5) |>    rayimage::plot_image() env = generate_sky_latlong(   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 18:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE ) rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure=-2) |>    rayimage::plot_image() env = generate_sky_latlong(   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 18:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   resolution = 800,   hosek = TRUE ) rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure=-4) |>    rayimage::plot_image() env = generate_sky_latlong(   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 18:37:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   verbose=TRUE,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE ) ## Sun: -4.1 elevation, 274.1 azimuth rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure=3) |>    rayimage::plot_image()

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: Note now ’re good portion atmosphere, start seeing scattered light us.
code: env = generate_sky_latlong(   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 12:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   altitude = 2000,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE ) rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure=-5) |>    rayimage::plot_image()

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: High enough altitudes majority scattered light coming viewer.
code: env = generate_sky_latlong(   filename    = NA,   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 12:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   altitude = 7500,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE ) rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure=-5) |>    rayimage::plot_image()

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: high altitudes, resulting render almost scattered light coming , resulting space-like appearance.
code: env = generate_sky_latlong(   filename    = NA,   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 12:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   altitude = 15000,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE ) rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure=-5) |>    rayimage::plot_image()

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: Note shadow Earth atmosphere opposite sun sun horizon.  Full sun + moon + stars (increased exposure artistic effect):
code: env = generate_sky_latlong(   filename    = NA,   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 18:30:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   altitude = 15000,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE )  rayimage::plot_image(env) env = generate_sky_latlong(   filename = NA,   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 18:37:00",tz="EST"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   resolution = 800,   hosek = FALSE,   stars = TRUE,   moon = TRUE,    stars_exposure = 12 ) rayimage::render_exposure(env, exposure = 2)  |>    rayimage::plot_image()

what: Custom sun position, multicore, atmosphere only
title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: skymodelr allows turn direct sun contribution included scattered light.
code: sky = generate_sky(   albedo = 0,   elevation = 25,   azimuth = 135,   resolution = 800,   number_cores = 2,   hosek = FALSE,   render_mode = "atmosphere" ) rayimage::render_exposure(sky, exposure=-6) |>    rayimage::plot_image()

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: generates places radiance-accurate moon scene, taking account moon’s phase, orientation respect viewer’s latitude longitude, surface albedo, non-lambertian reflectivity, distance earth, earthshine, “opposition surge” effect, attenuation due atmosphere varying lunar elevations. moon rendered rayvertex resampled correct size environment map. pre-scaled renders.
code: moon_sky = generate_moon_latlong(   filename   = NA,   datetime  = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 02:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat       = 38.9072,   lon       = -77.0369,   albedo    = 0.2,   turbidity = 3,   resolution = 6000,   number_cores = 8,   moon_atmosphere = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE ) #Increase exposure moon_sky |>    rayimage::render_exposure(10) |>    rayimage::plot_image() moon_image1 = skymodelr:::generate_moon_image_latlong(datetime  = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 02:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat       = 38.9072,   lon       = -77.0369) rayimage::render_exposure(moon_image1$moon_luminance_array, 2, preview = TRUE) #Change latitude and our orientation changes moon_image2 = skymodelr:::generate_moon_image_latlong(datetime  = as.POSIXct("2025-03-10 02:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat       = 38.9072,   lon       = -77.0369) rayimage::render_exposure(moon_image2$moon_luminance_array, 2, preview = TRUE) moon_image3 = skymodelr:::generate_moon_image_latlong(datetime  = as.POSIXct("2025-03-10 02:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat       = 0,   lon       = -77.0369) rayimage::render_exposure(moon_image3$moon_luminance_array, 2, preview = TRUE) moon_image4 = skymodelr:::generate_moon_image_latlong(datetime  = as.POSIXct("2025-03-10 02:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat       = -38.9072,   lon       = -77.0369) rayimage::render_exposure(moon_image4$moon_luminance_array, 2, preview = TRUE)

what: Star field aligned to time and place
title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: Stars rendered using color values derived star’s temperature.  Now render entire sphere:
code: stars = generate_stars(   datetime  = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 02:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat       = 38.9072,   lon       = -77.0369,   resolution = 800,   atmosphere_effects = TRUE,   upper_hemisphere_only = TRUE ) stars |>    rayimage::render_exposure(16) |>    rayimage::plot_image() stars_full = generate_stars(   datetime  = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 02:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat       = 38.9072,   lon       = -77.0369,   resolution = 800,   atmosphere_effects = FALSE,   upper_hemisphere_only = FALSE ) stars_full |>    rayimage::render_exposure(16) |>    rayimage::plot_image()

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
code: # Download once (choose variant via args): coef_path = download_sky_data(sea_level = TRUE, wide_spectrum = FALSE)  # Render with the Prague model: sky_prague = generate_sky(   albedo = 0.3,   elevation = 10,   azimuth = 90,   altitude = 0,   hosek = FALSE,   wide_spectrum = FALSE,   visibility = 50,   resolution = 2048,   number_cores = 4 ) plot_image(sky_prague)

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: can pass EXR images rayrender. ’s daytime sky:  sunset sky, sun coming due North, +Z rayrender. trying match real place, make adjustments existing environment map’s orientation using rotate_env argument rayrender.
code: library(rayrender) day_exr = tempfile(fileext=".exr") generate_sky(   filename=day_exr,   elevation = 20,    azimuth = 135,   resolution = 1000,   hosek = FALSE )  generate_ground(depth = -0.5, material=diffuse(color="grey20", checkercolor = "grey50"),spheresize = 10000) |>   add_object(obj_model(r_obj())) |>    render_scene(environment_light = day_exr, iso=10,                 clamp_value = 1000, fov = 80, lookfrom = c(0,0,-1), aperture = 0.05) sunset_exr = tempfile(fileext=".exr") generate_sky(   filename=sunset_exr,   elevation = 4,    azimuth = 0,   resolution = 2000,   hosek = FALSE )  #This has the light coming from north generate_ground(depth = -0.4, material=diffuse(color="grey20", checkercolor = "grey50"),spheresize = 10000) |>   add_object(obj_model(r_obj())) |>    render_scene(environment_light = sunset_exr, iso=10,                 clamp_value = 1000, fov = 80, lookfrom = c(0,0,-1), aperture = 0.05) #Rotate the existing env 225 degrees to come from the southwest. generate_ground(depth = -0.4, material=diffuse(color="grey20", checkercolor = "grey50"),spheresize = 10000) |>   add_object(obj_model(r_obj())) |>    render_scene(environment_light = sunset_exr, iso=20,                 clamp_value = 10000, fov = 40, lookfrom = c(0,1,-4), aperture = 0.05,                 rotate_env = 225)

title: Generates and Samples Realistic Terrestrial Atmospheres
text: Hosek–Wilkie sky model (analytic daylight model). Prague 2021–22 spectral sky model (wide‑spectrum, ‑horizon sun, altitude). Star positions Yale Bright Star Catalog.

code: altaz_to_enu(alt_rad, azN_rad)

title: Convert altitude/azimuth to ENU — altaz_to_enu
text: alt_rad Altitude radians. azN_rad Azimuth radians measured north.

what: Sample a direction from the Prague model. — calculate_sky_values
title: Sample a direction from the Prague model. — calculate_sky_values
text: Evaluate Prague spectral sky model arbitrary spherical directions without writing image, returning radiance-samples.

title: Sample a direction from the Prague model. — calculate_sky_values
code: calculate_sky_values(   phi,   theta,   altitude = 0,   elevation = 10,   visibility = 50,   albedo = 0.5,   azimuth = 90,   number_cores = 1,   wide_spectrum = FALSE,   render_mode = "all" )

title: Sample a direction from the Prague model. — calculate_sky_values
text: phi Horizontal angle sample, degrees. Vectorized. Range 0 360. theta Vertical angle sample, degrees. Vectorized. Range -90 90. altitude Default 0, vectorized. Altitude viewer meters. Range 0 15000. elevation Default 10, vectorized. Solar elevation angle /horizon (degrees). Range -4.2 90. visibility Default 50, vectorized. Range 20 131.8. Meteorological range kilometers Prague model. albedo Default 0.5, vectorized. Range 0 1. Ground albedo. azimuth Default 90, single value. Solar azimuth (degrees). Defaults South. number_cores Default 1. Number threads use computation. wide_spectrum Default FALSE. Whether use wide-spectrum (55-channel, polarised) coefficients. render_mode Default "". One "", "atmosphere", "sun". Use "" atmosphere + solar disk, "atmosphere" atmospheric radiance , "sun" solar disk .

title: Sample a direction from the Prague model. — calculate_sky_values
text: 3-column RGB matrix.

title: Sample a direction from the Prague model. — calculate_sky_values
code: # Generate a basic atmosphere with the Prague model if(run_documentation()) { value_grid = expand.grid(   phi = seq(0,360,by=30),   theta = seq(0,90,by=10),   altitude = c(0,10000)  ) vals = calculate_sky_values(   phi = value_grid$phi,   theta = value_grid$theta,   altitude = value_grid$altitude,   elevation = 45,   visibility = 120,   albedo = 0  )  cbind(value_grid, vals) } #>  Coefficient file for this setting not yet present: this is a large file (2.4GB), download? [y/n]  #> Error in check_coef_file("SkyModelDataset.dat"): Input not recognized.

what: Sample sun luminance at the disk center. — calculate_sun_brightness
title: Sample sun luminance at the disk center. — calculate_sun_brightness
text: Evaluate Prague Hosek sky model sun center integrate CIE Y curve return luminance value. useful relative attenuation (e.g., comparing zenith sun low sun).

title: Sample sun luminance at the disk center. — calculate_sun_brightness
code: calculate_sun_brightness(   elevation = 10,   azimuth = 90,   albedo = 0.5,   turbidity = 3,   altitude = 0,   visibility = 50,   hosek = TRUE,   wide_spectrum = FALSE,   lambda_nm = NULL )

title: Sample sun luminance at the disk center. — calculate_sun_brightness
text: elevation Default 10. Solar elevation angle /horizon (degrees). Range [-4.2, 90] Prague, [0, 90] Hosek. azimuth Default 90. Solar azimuth (degrees). albedo Default 0.5. Ground albedo, range 0 1. turbidity Default 3. Atmospheric turbidity, range 1.7 10 (Hosek ). altitude Default 0. Observer altitude (m), range 0 15000 (Prague ). visibility Default 50. Meteorological range (km); Prague . hosek Default TRUE. FALSE selects Prague model. wide_spectrum Default FALSE. Use wide-spectrum (55-channel) coefficients Prague sea level . lambda_nm Optional vector wavelengths Hosek sampling.

title: Sample sun luminance at the disk center. — calculate_sun_brightness
text: Numeric scalar sun luminance (CIE Y, relative scale).

title: Sample sun luminance at the disk center. — calculate_sun_brightness
code: if(run_documentation()) {   calculate_sun_brightness(elevation = 45, hosek = TRUE) } #> [1] 2237321

what: Download Prague Sky Model Coefficient Data — download_sky_data
title: Download Prague Sky Model Coefficient Data — download_sky_data
text: model allows sun angles horizon, wide spectral ranges including infrared, polarization, well rendering altitude) need download coefficient file. three versions dataset, listed order increasing size:

title: Download Prague Sky Model Coefficient Data — download_sky_data
code: download_sky_data(sea_level = TRUE, wide_spectrum = FALSE)

title: Download Prague Sky Model Coefficient Data — download_sky_data
text: sea_level Default TRUE. Download sea‑level–data. Set FALSE download full‑altitude dataset. wide_spectrum Default FALSE. TRUE, downloads wide‑spectrum (55‑channel, polarised) version. Valid sea_level = TRUE.

title: Download Prague Sky Model Coefficient Data — download_sky_data
text: Invisibly, full path data file.

title: Download Prague Sky Model Coefficient Data — download_sky_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Standard (11‑channel, sea‑level) coefficients   download_sky_data()    # Wide‑spectrum sea‑level coefficients   download_sky_data(wide_spectrum = TRUE)    # Full altitude‑range coefficients   download_sky_data(sea_level = FALSE) } # }

what: Generate the moon into a lat-long image array patch — generate_moon_image_latlong
title: Generate the moon into a lat-long image array patch — generate_moon_image_latlong
text: Produce rasterised moon texture aligned composition sky dome. Earth phase inferred Sun-Moon geometry, earthshine implemented emissive term scaled relative solar irradiance.

title: Generate the moon into a lat-long image array patch — generate_moon_image_latlong
code: generate_moon_image_latlong(   datetime,   lat,   lon,   elev_m = 0,   width = 800,   height = 800,   earthshine = TRUE,   earthshine_albedo = 0.19,   solar_irradiance_w_m2 = 1300,   moon_extinction_kV = 0.172,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Generate the moon into a lat-long image array patch — generate_moon_image_latlong
text: datetime POSIXct observation time (UTC). lat Latitude degrees. lon Longitude degrees. elev_m Observer elevation sea level metres. width Output width pixels. height Output height pixels. earthshine Default TRUE. FALSE, skip earthshine emissive term. earthshine_albedo Default 0.19. Effective Earth albedo term used earthshine irradiance approximation. solar_irradiance_w_m2 Default 1300. Reference solar irradiance 1 AU (W/m^2) used normalize earthshine emission intensity. moon_extinction_kV Default 0.172. V-band atmospheric extinction coefficient direct moonlight attenuation. verbose Default FALSE. TRUE, print earthshine diagnostics.

what: Generate the atmosphere with the moon — generate_moon_latlong
title: Generate the atmosphere with the moon — generate_moon_latlong
text: Note just scaled version generate_sky(), scaled luminance moon compared sun. function takes phase moon account, along increase luminosity around full moon (known opposition surge). Moonlight attenuation uses Rozenberg/Krisciunas-Schaefer airmass approximation configurable moon_extinction_kV.

title: Generate the atmosphere with the moon — generate_moon_latlong
code: generate_moon_latlong(   datetime,   lat,   lon,   filename = NA,   albedo = 0.5,   turbidity = 3,   altitude = 0,   resolution = 2048,   number_cores = 1,   moon_atmosphere = FALSE,   earthshine = TRUE,   earthshine_albedo = 0.19,   solar_irradiance_w_m2 = 1300,   moon_extinction_kV = 0.172,   hosek = TRUE,   wide_spectrum = FALSE,   visibility = 50,   moon_texture_width = 801,   moon_texture_height = 801,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Generate the atmosphere with the moon — generate_moon_latlong
text: datetime POSIX-compatible date-time. lat Observer latitude (degrees N). lon Observer longitude (degrees E; west < 0). filename Default NA. Path image file write. albedo Default 0.5. Ground albedo, range 0 1. turbidity Default 3. Atmospheric turbidity, range 1.7 10 (Hosek ). altitude Default 0. Observer altitude (m), range 0 15000 (Prague ). resolution Default 2048. Image height pixels (width = 2 * height). number_cores Default 1. CPU threads use. moon_atmosphere Default FALSE. TRUE, generates atmospheric scattering light moon. earthshine Default TRUE. FALSE, disable earthshine contribution moon. earthshine_albedo Default 0.19. Effective Earth albedo term used earthshine irradiance approximation. solar_irradiance_w_m2 Default 1300. Reference solar irradiance 1 AU (W/m^2) used normalize earthshine emission intensity. moon_extinction_kV Default 0.172. V-band atmospheric extinction coefficient direct moonlight attenuation. hosek Default TRUE. FALSE selects Prague model. wide_spectrum Default FALSE. 55-channel Prague coefficients (altitude = 0m ). visibility Default 50. Meteorological range (km); Prague . moon_texture_width Default 801. Internal moon-texture render width. moon_texture_height Default 801. Internal moon-texture render height. verbose Default FALSE. Whether print progress bars/diagnostic info.

title: Generate the atmosphere with the moon — generate_moon_latlong
text: Either image array, array invisibly returned file written. array dimensions (resolution, 2 * resolution, 4).

title: Generate the atmosphere with the moon — generate_moon_latlong
text: Writing non-EXR formats introduce precision loss HDR data quantised destination format, low dynamic range outputs like PNG JPEG files represent true luminosity values encoded array.

title: Generate the atmosphere with the moon — generate_moon_latlong
code: # Moonlit sky (Hosek), mid-evening in DC if(run_documentation()) { generate_moon_latlong(   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-09-05 19:30:00",tz="America/New_York"),   lat        = 38.9072,   lon        = -77.0369,   resolution = 400,   turbidity  = 3,   verbose    = TRUE ) |>   rayimage::render_exposure(15) |>   rayimage::plot_image() } #> Error: 'render_exposure' is not an exported object from 'namespace:rayimage'

what: Generate a bright-planet image array — generate_planets
title: Generate a bright-planet image array — generate_planets
text: Build planetary luminance map aligned sky dome compositing within generate_sky_latlong().

title: Generate a bright-planet image array — generate_planets
code: generate_planets(   datetime,   lon,   lat,   filename = NA,   resolution = 2048,   turbidity = 3,   ozone_du = 300,   altitude = 0,   color = FALSE,   planet_width = 1,   upper_hemisphere_only = TRUE,   atmosphere_effects = TRUE,   number_cores = 1,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Generate a bright-planet image array — generate_planets
text: datetime POSIXct timestamp used ephemerides. lon Observer longitude degrees (east positive). lat Observer latitude degrees. filename Default NA. Destination image path write. NA, image array returned without writing. resolution Default 2048. Map half-width (image 2 * resolution × resolution). turbidity Atmospheric turbidity extinction modelling. ozone_du Column ozone (Dobson Units) colour shifts. altitude Observer altitude metres. color Render RGB (TRUE) stars monochrome (FALSE). planet_width Approximate point-spread size planets pixels. upper_hemisphere_only TRUE, mask pixels horizon. atmosphere_effects TRUE, apply atmospheric extinction. number_cores CPU threads used rendering. verbose Emit diagnostic output TRUE.

title: Generate a bright-planet image array — generate_planets
text: Either image array, array invisibly returned file written. array dimensions (resolution, 2 * resolution, 4).

title: Generate a bright-planet image array — generate_planets
text: Writing non-EXR formats introduce precision loss HDR data quantised destination format, low dynamic range outputs like PNG JPEG files represent true luminosity values encoded array.

title: Generate a bright-planet image array — generate_planets
code: # Basic star field over Washington, DC at a fixed time if(run_documentation()) { generate_planets(   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 02:20:00", tz = "EST"),   lon        = -77.0369,   lat        = 38.9072,   resolution = 400,   color      = TRUE,   planet_width = 1,   atmosphere_effects   = TRUE,   upper_hemisphere_only = TRUE,   number_cores = 2 ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() }

what: Generate a Hosek-Wilkie sky dome array — generate_sky
title: Generate a Hosek-Wilkie sky dome array — generate_sky
text: Evaluate either Hosek-Wilkie Prague analytic sky models return high-dynamic-range image array given solar configuration. image file written filename supplied.

title: Generate a Hosek-Wilkie sky dome array — generate_sky
code: generate_sky(   filename = NA,   albedo = 0.1,   turbidity = 3,   elevation = 10,   azimuth = 90,   altitude = 0,   resolution = 2048,   number_cores = 1,   hosek = TRUE,   wide_spectrum = FALSE,   visibility = 50,   verbose = FALSE,   render_mode = "all",   below_horizon = TRUE )

title: Generate a Hosek-Wilkie sky dome array — generate_sky
text: filename Default NA. Path image file write. given, array returned instead. albedo Default 0.1. 0.0-1.0 ground albedo. Grass albedo 0.09, landscape covered snow albedo 1.0. turbidity Default 3. 1.7-10 atmospheric turbidity. valid Hosek model. elevation Default 10. Solar elevation horizon (degrees). azimuth Default 90, sun directly east. Solar azimuth (degrees). left edge image faces north middle faces south. altitude Default 0. Altitude viewer meters. Valid range: 0 15000. valid Prague model. resolution Default 2048. Height image. Width twice number. number_cores Default 1. Number threads use computation. hosek Default TRUE. Set FALSE enable Prague 2021-22 spectral sky model. wide_spectrum Default FALSE. Use 55-channel Prague coefficients (sea level ). visibility Default 50. Meteorological range kilometres Prague model. verbose Default FALSE. Whether print progress bars/diagnostic info. render_mode Default "". One "", "atmosphere", "sun". Use "" atmosphere + solar disk, "atmosphere" atmospheric radiance , "sun" solar disk . below_horizon Default TRUE. Whether sample atmospheric scattering horizon, non-zero altitude > 0.

title: Generate a Hosek-Wilkie sky dome array — generate_sky
text: Either image array, array invisibly returned file written. array dimensions (resolution, 2 * resolution, 4).

title: Generate a Hosek-Wilkie sky dome array — generate_sky
text: Writing non-EXR formats introduce precision loss HDR data quantised destination format, low dynamic range outputs like PNG JPEG files represent true luminosity values encoded array.

title: Generate a Hosek-Wilkie sky dome array — generate_sky
code: if(run_documentation()) { # Hosek model (default): clear morning, Sun SE, with solar disk generate_sky(   resolution = 400,   elevation  = 15,   azimuth    = 135,   turbidity  = 3,   render_mode = "all" ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() }  if(run_documentation()) { # Same view but hazier and without the solar disk generate_sky(   resolution = 400,   elevation  = 15,   azimuth    = 135,   turbidity  = 6,   render_mode = "atmosphere" ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() }  # Prague model (may prompt to download coefficients on first use) # \donttest{ if(run_documentation()) { generate_sky(   resolution = 400,   hosek      = FALSE,   altitude   = 0,   visibility = 80,   albedo     = 0.2,   elevation  = 5,   azimuth    = 220,   number_cores = 2 ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() } #>  Coefficient file for this setting not yet present: this is a large file (107MB), download? [y/n]  #> Error in check_coef_file("SkyModelDatasetGround.dat"): Input not recognized. # }

what: Generate a location and time-specific sky dome (optionally with stars) — generate_sky_latlong
title: Generate a location and time-specific sky dome (optionally with stars) — generate_sky_latlong
text: Convenience wrapper around generate_sky() : Computes Sun's apparent position datetime, lat, lon (via Swiss Ephemeris / swephR). Renders corresponding sky model. Optionally overlays star field using generate_stars() moon generate_moon_latlong().

title: Generate a location and time-specific sky dome (optionally with stars) — generate_sky_latlong
code: generate_sky_latlong(   datetime,   lat,   lon,   filename = NA,   albedo = 0.5,   turbidity = 3,   altitude = 0,   resolution = 2048,   number_cores = 1,   hosek = TRUE,   wide_spectrum = FALSE,   visibility = 50,   stars = FALSE,   star_width = 1,   planets = FALSE,   moon = FALSE,   moon_atmosphere = FALSE,   moon_hosek = TRUE,   render_mode = "all",   below_horizon = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   stars_exposure = 0,   ... )

title: Generate a location and time-specific sky dome (optionally with stars) — generate_sky_latlong
text: datetime POSIX-compatible date-time. lat Observer latitude (degrees N). lon Observer longitude (degrees E; west < 0). filename Default NA. Path .exr file write. albedo Default 0.5. Ground albedo, range 0 1. turbidity Default 3. Atmospheric turbidity, range 1.7 10 (Hosek ). altitude Default 0. Observer altitude (m), range 0 15000 (Prague ). resolution Default 2048. Image height pixels (width = 2 * height). number_cores Default 1. CPU threads use. hosek Default TRUE. FALSE selects Prague model. wide_spectrum Default FALSE. 55-channel Prague coefficients (altitude = 0m ). visibility Default 50. Meteorological range (km); Prague . stars Default FALSE. TRUE, composite star field using generate_stars(). Automatically falls back pure star render black-sky condition met (see Details). star_width Default 1. Passed generate_stars() control stellar point-spread size. planets Default FALSE. TRUE, composite bright planets via generate_planets(). moon Default FALSE. TRUE, overlay moon render generate_moon_latlong(). moon_atmosphere Default FALSE. TRUE, generates atmospheric scattering light moon. moon_hosek Default TRUE. Whether use faster (less accurate) Hosek model atmospheric scattering moon. Note light scattered moon much less intense sun, thus small inaccuracies much less likely noticable. render_mode Default "". One "", "atmosphere", "sun". Use "" atmosphere + solar disk, "atmosphere" atmospheric radiance , "sun" solar disk . below_horizon Default TRUE. Whether sample atmospheric scattering horizon, non-zero altitude > 0. verbose Default FALSE. Whether print progress bars/diagnostic info. stars_exposure Default 0. Increases star exposure 2^exposure. Non-physical, just controls adjustments artistic effect. ... Additional named arguments forwarded generate_stars(), enabled, generate_planets() generate_moon_latlong(). Unmatched names ignored.

title: Generate a location and time-specific sky dome (optionally with stars) — generate_sky_latlong
text: Either raw data, data invisibly returned filename given. EXR written filename.

title: Generate a location and time-specific sky dome (optionally with stars) — generate_sky_latlong
text: Solar angles - altitude (degrees horizon) azimuth (degrees clockwise east, 90 degrees = south) - derived internally; never supply directly. Black-sky rule - Prague model sky radiance defined -4.2 degrees, Hosek model defined 0 degrees. function skips sky render writes stars stars = TRUE.

title: Generate a location and time-specific sky dome (optionally with stars) — generate_sky_latlong
code: # Morning sunrise on spring solstice over Washington, DC with Prague model if(run_documentation()) { generate_sky_latlong(   datetime    = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 06:15:00",tz="EST"),   lat         = 38.9072,   lon         = -77.0369,   number_cores = 2,   hosek = FALSE ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() } #>  Coefficient file for this setting not yet present: this is a large file (107MB), download? [y/n]  #> Error in check_coef_file("SkyModelDatasetGround.dat"): Input not recognized. if(run_documentation()) { generate_sky_latlong(   datetime    = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 12:00:00",tz="EST"),   lat         = 38.9072,   lon         = -77.0369,   number_cores = 2, ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() }  if(run_documentation()) { generate_sky_latlong(   datetime    = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 18:00:00",tz="EST"),   lat         = 38.9072,   lon         = -77.0369,   number_cores = 2, ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() }  if(run_documentation()) { generate_sky_latlong(   datetime    = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 18:30:00",tz="EST"),   lat         = 38.9072,   lon         = -77.0369,   number_cores = 2,   hosek = FALSE,   verbose=TRUE, ) |>   rayimage::render_exposure(exposure=2) |>   rayimage::plot_image() } #> Error: 'render_exposure' is not an exported object from 'namespace:rayimage'

what: Generate a star‑field array aligned with generate_sky() — generate_stars
title: Generate a star‑field array aligned with generate_sky() — generate_stars
text: Render star map given observer location, time, atmospheric conditions can composited generate_sky(). Returns (resolution, 2 * resolution, 4) array opaque alpha channel. image file written filename supplied.

title: Generate a star‑field array aligned with generate_sky() — generate_stars
code: generate_stars(   lon,   lat,   datetime,   filename = NA,   resolution = 2048,   turbidity = 3,   ozone_du = 300,   altitude = 0,   color = TRUE,   star_width = 1,   upper_hemisphere_only = TRUE,   atmosphere_effects = TRUE,   number_cores = 1 )

title: Generate a star‑field array aligned with generate_sky() — generate_stars
text: lon Observer longitude degrees (east positive). lat Observer latitude degrees. datetime POSIXct timestamp used compute local sidereal time. filename Default NA. Path image file write. NA, image array returned without writing. resolution Default 2048. Map half-width; output image 2 * resolution × resolution. turbidity Default 3.0. Atmospheric turbidity controlling aerosol optical depth extinction/reddening. ozone_du Default 300.0. Column ozone Dobson Units used atmospheric absorption. altitude Default 0.0. Observer altitude mean sea level meters. color Default TRUE. TRUE, render RGB star colors; FALSE, render monochrome luminance. star_width Default 1. Approximate stellar point-spread size pixels (controls apparent star sharpness). upper_hemisphere_only Default TRUE. TRUE, pixels local horizon suppressed match generate_sky()’s visible hemisphere. atmosphere_effects Default TRUE. TRUE, apply atmospheric extinction color shift using turbidity, ozone_du, altitude. number_cores Default 1. Number CPU threads use.

title: Generate a star‑field array aligned with generate_sky() — generate_stars
text: Either image array, array invisibly returned file written. array dimensions (resolution, 2 * resolution, 4).

title: Generate a star‑field array aligned with generate_sky() — generate_stars
text: Writing non-EXR formats introduce precision loss HDR data quantised destination format, low dynamic range outputs like PNG JPEG files represent true luminosity values encoded array.

title: Generate a star‑field array aligned with generate_sky() — generate_stars
code: # Note: exposure has been increased for all examples (via white_point) for # ease of visibility in documentation  # Basic star field over Washington, DC at a fixed time if(run_documentation()) { generate_stars(   resolution = 400,   lon        = -77.0369,   lat        = 38.9072,   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-03-21 02:20:00", tz = "EST"),   color      = TRUE,   star_width = 1,   atmosphere_effects   = TRUE,   upper_hemisphere_only = TRUE,   number_cores = 2 ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() }  if(run_documentation()) { # Monochrome stars, no atmospheric extinction/reddening, full sphere generate_stars(   resolution = 400,   lon        = -122.4194,   lat        = 37.7749,   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-06-01 08:00:00", tz = "UTC"),   color      = FALSE,   star_width = 1,   upper_hemisphere_only = FALSE,   atmosphere_effects    = FALSE ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() }  if(run_documentation()) { # Sharper stars (smaller PSF) with ozone/turbidity and altitude generate_stars(   resolution = 400,   lon        = 10,   lat        = 45,   datetime   = as.POSIXct("2025-12-01 22:00:00", tz = "UTC"),   star_width = 0.5,   turbidity  = 3.5,   ozone_du   = 320,   altitude   = 1000,   color      = TRUE ) |>   rayimage::plot_image() }
